title: rbac — authorization policies
description: Four policies. RolePolicy covers CRUD on tenant + platform role rows. PermissionPolicy covers read-only for tenant admins + platform-admin CRUD. RoleAssignmentPolicy is separate because assignment carries its own gate (an admin can VIEW every role but can only ASSIGN roles whose permissions the actor themselves holds). RoleDefinitionPolicy covers the platform-admin metadata layer.

abilities.scope: any tenant member sees the role list applicable to the tenant
tenant-scoped: refuses when role.tenant_id is set AND != caller.tenant_id
owner + admin. Refused when tenant lacks custom_roles entitlement OR quota exhausted
owner + admin on tenant-owned roles. Refused for is_system=true rows.
owner + admin on tenant-owned custom roles. Refused when active assignments exist.
owner + admin. Enforces the RoleHasPermissionsObserver rules — guard + application boundaries + max_permissions_per_role.
same as attachPermission.
cross-tenant, cross-Application — shows every system + custom role in the DB
platform-plane owned super_admin + product. Compliance-audited.
same as platformCreate. Refuses guard_name / application_id changes on rows with active assignments.
super_admin only. Compliance-audited.
tenant admins see the full catalogue
Enterprise-only, requires custom_permissions entitlement
cross-Application — the full permission catalogue
super_admin + product. Usually PermissionCatalogueLoader creates permissions automatically — this policy gates manual writes.
owner + admin see every principal's assignments in the tenant
any authenticated user sees THEIR OWN assignments. Backs GET /me/roles.
owner + admin. Enforces role_assignable_by_actor — an admin can only assign roles whose permission set the admin themselves already holds. Prevents privilege escalation.
owner + admin. Same privilege-escalation guard — can only revoke roles whose permission set the actor holds.
owner + admin, requires bulk_role_assignment entitlement
super_admin + product. Creates the metadata row for a new system role.
same. Updating default_permissions triggers re-seed of affected tenants (config-gated).

boundary_note: Every ability respects the two-audience guard boundary. Tenant-guard abilities scope to the caller's own tenant (application_id + tenant_id filter enforced at the policy). Platform-guard abilities are cross-tenant, cross-Application. Neither leaks into the other's map — cross-guard checks return false silently. Design.md §2 D-A2 GuardMismatch is the write-time counterpart to this read-time policy split.
